note.soap:
S: A 35-year-old adult female reports a one-week history of burning urination without associated fever, flank pain, or vaginal discharge. She denies any systemic symptoms such as chills or malaise. Patient is not pregnant. There are no known medication allergies.

O: No physical exam available due to phone-only encounter. Vital signs and physical findings cannot be assessed remotely. No laboratory or imaging results are available at this time.

A:  
  - Working diagnosis (most likely): Uncomplicated lower urinary tract infection (cystitis)  
  - Differential: Urethritis (non-infectious or sexually transmitted), vaginitis, interstitial cystitis  
  - Red flags considered: Absence of fever, flank pain, or systemic symptoms reduces concern for pyelonephritis or complicated UTI.

P:  
  - Disposition: Manage as uncomplicated UTI in urgent care setting with close follow-up if symptoms worsen or new symptoms develop.  
  - Plan: Empiric antibiotic therapy with nitrofurantoin 100 mg twice daily for 5 days is recommended, assuming no contraindications. Alternatively, trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole may be considered if local resistance patterns allow and no allergies exist. Encourage increased fluid intake and symptomatic relief with phenazopyridine if needed. Advise patient to seek in-person evaluation if fever, flank pain, worsening symptoms, or vaginal discharge develops.  
  - Safety net: Return for urgent care or primary care evaluation if symptoms persist beyond 48–72 hours after starting antibiotics, or if new systemic symptoms such as fever or flank pain occur.

Evidence (snippets used):  
- Duration: 1 week  
- Fever: no  
- Flank pain: no  
- Pregnancy: no  
- Discharge: no
patient_instructions: If you are not improving as expected, or if symptoms worsen, seek in-person medical care.
Medication options you may consider if safe (no dosing by phone): acetaminophen (Tylenol) if you can take it safely; ibuprofen (Advil) if you can take it safely.

evidence.title: IDSA Guideline: Uncomplicated Cystitis and Pyelonephritis
